speaker: Kristin Neff
episode_or_content_title: The Space Between Self-Esteem and Self Compassion — TEDxCentennialParkWomen
verification_notes: Created 2026-05-26 via the Six-State Buildout (Phase 8 human-gated promotion). prototype_only reconstruction — paraphrase of the speaker's documented idea; NOT a verified verbatim excerpt. Kristin Neff, PhD, is an Associate Professor at the University of Texas at Austin and one of the world's leading researchers on self-compassion. The TEDxCentennialParkWomen talk "The Space Between Self-Esteem and Self Compassion" is an official TED-affiliated event; a full transcript is publicly available at singjupost.com. Key passages verified from the transcript: "What happens when we fail? We feel lousy, we feel terrible about ourselves"; "to be human means to be imperfect"; "treating ourselves like we treat a good friend"; "it's there for you precisely when you fail — just when self-esteem deserts you, self-compassion steps in." Tier-1 credibility (published researcher, peer-reviewed work). Quote type: paraphrase from verified public transcript of official TEDx event.
